medical_history.chief_complaint: Pathologically increasing head circumference and symptoms of increased intracranial pressure, in combination with marked developmental delay and loss of abilities
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: At 1 month of age, she underwent an echocardiogram revealing a persistent foramen ovale but no cardiac insufficiency and also had two suspected left partial epileptic seizures. At 2 months, developmental delay was noted with lack of eye contact, no smiles, and feeding difficulties, and at 3.5 months, an increase in head circumference was noted. At 6 months of age, she had opisthotonus-like attacks with an increasing frequency. She also kept her head extended when placed in bed, all signs corresponding to symptoms of increased intracranial pressure.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Persistent foramen ovale, suspected left partial epileptic seizures, developmental delay, feeding difficulties
medical_history.family_history: Mother with similar cutaneous capillary haemangiomas and RASA1 mutation

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Radiological evaluation with transfonatanellar ultrasound and MRI revealed a vein of Galen malformation with hydrocephalus, and tonsillar prolapse. Angiographic examination revealed a mural type of vein of Galen malformation with its main supply from the postero-lateral and postero-medial choroidal arteries. There was a pronounced hypoplasia of the superior sagittal sinus compensated by enlarged cortical veins draining mainly into the cavernous sinus. Persisting occipital and marginal sinuses were noted and there was a high-grade stenosis of the right jugular bulb.
